changes-in-inventories.description: The stocks of goods held by firms in Slovakia decreased by 1.70 EUR Billion in the fourth quarter of 2025. consumer-confidence.source_name: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic
consumer-confidence.description: Slovakia’s consumer confidence indicator edged up by 0.8 percentage points to -27.4 in April 2026 from -28.2 in the previous month, which was the lowest in three years, signaling a slight improvement in household sentiment. Consumers were less pessimistic about their ability to save (-14.1 vs -16.1 in March), while concerns over future unemployment also eased marginally (37.1 vs 38.8). Conversely,

business-confidence.source_name: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic
business-confidence.description: The industrial confidence indicator in Slovakia rose by 5.6 points month-on-month to 2.3 in April 2026, rebounding from -3.3 in the previous month and remained at its highest level since December 2025. corporate-tax-rate.description: The Corporate Tax Rate in Slovakia stands at 24 percent. Corporate Tax Rate in Slovakia averaged 25.56 percent from 1993 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 45.00 percent in 1993 and a record low of 19.00 percent in 2004. source: Tax Directorate, Slovakia

consumer-spending.source_name: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic
consumer-spending.description: Consumer Spending in Slovakia increased to 15.10 EUR Billion in the fourth quarter of 2025 from 15.03 EUR Billion in the third quarter of 2025. corruption-index.description: Slovakia scored 48 points out of 100 on the 2025 Corruption Perceptions Index reported by Transparency International. Corruption Index in Slovakia averaged 45.68 Points from 1998 until 2025, reaching an all time high of 54.00 Points in 2023 and a record low of 35.00 Points in 2000. source: Transparency International

capacity-utilization.description: Capacity Utilization in Slovakia decreased to 79.10 percent in the first quarter of 2026 from 80.10 percent in the fourth quarter of 2025. corruption-rank.description: Slovakia is the 61 least corrupt nation out of 180 countries, according to the 2025 Corruption Perceptions Index reported by Transparency International. Corruption Rank in Slovakia averaged 54.71 from 1998 until 2025, reaching an all time high of 66.00 in 2011 and a record low of 47.00 in 1998. source: Transparency International

Disposable Personal Income in Slovakia averaged 10119.05 EUR Million from 1995 until 2025, reaching an all time high of 22526.81 EUR Million in the fourth quarter of 2025 and a record low of 2607.31 EUR Million in the first quarter of 1995.

current-account.description: Slovakia’s current account deficit narrowed to EUR 397.9 million in February 2026 from EUR 564.2 million in the corresponding month in the previous year. This marked the smallest deficit since October 2025. The goods account swung to a surplus of EUR 41.7 million from a shortfall of EUR 136.5 million, while the primary income deficit narrowed to EUR 334.1 million from EUR 339.9 million. However, t

exports.description: Exports in Slovakia increased by 0.5% year-on-year to EUR 10.1 billion in March 2026, driven largely by an almost 15% jump in shipments of miscellaneous manufactured articles. Additionally, sales to the EU grew nearly 4%, while sales to non-EU countries declined by almost 10%. source: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic

gdp-per-capita-ppp.description: The Gross Domestic Product per capita in Slovakia was last recorded at 40346.57 US dollars in 2024, when adjusted by purchasing power parity (PPP). The GDP per Capita, in Slovakia, when adjusted by Purchasing Power Parity is equivalent to 227 percent of the world's average. source: World Bank

gdp-per-capita.description: The Gross Domestic Product per capita in Slovakia was last recorded at 19797.87 US dollars in 2024. The GDP per Capita in Slovakia is equivalent to 157 percent of the world's average. gdp.description: The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in Slovakia was worth 141.78 billion US dollars in 2024, according to official data from the World Bank. The GDP value of Slovakia represents 0.13 percent of the world economy. source: World Bank

hospital-beds.description: Hospital Beds in Slovakia decreased to 5.66 per 1000 people in 2023 from 5.69 per 1000 people in 2022. Hospital Beds in Slovakia averaged 6.60 per 1000 people from 1996 until 2023, reaching an all time high of 8.32 per 1000 people in 1996 and a record low of 5.66 per 1000 people in 2023. source: OECD

imports.source_name: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic
imports.description: Imports in Slovakia dropped by 1.6% year-on-year to EUR 9.6 billion in March 2026, due to continued declines across most product categories, specifically in five out of ten sectors. Inbound shipments fell most sharply for machinery and transport equipment, down roughly 4%. Imports from EU countries increased by nearly 5%, while arrivals from non-EU markets were lower by 13% <span class='source-pre

industrial-production.source_name: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic
industrial-production.description: Industrial production in Slovakia fell 0.9% year-on-year in March 2026, improving from a 2.9% drop in February. The easing in the pace of decline was partly driven by a smaller drag from key industries, even as weakness persisted in core manufacturing sectors. The manufacture of transport equipment remained the main negative contributor, falling nearly 6% for a second consecutive month. Refined pe

inflation-cpi.source_name: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic
inflation-cpi.description: Slovakia’s annual inflation rate rose to 3.9% in April 2026 from 3.5% in March, slightly above market expectations of 3.8%. It marked the highest reading since January 2026, driven mainly by a sharp acceleration in housing and utilities, which rose 6.3% amid regulatory adjustments, particularly higher heating energy prices (27.8%) and gas (6.7%). Additionally, transport costs climbed to an over th

population.source_name: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic
population.description: The total population in Slovakia was estimated at 5.4 million people in 2025, according to the latest census figures and projections from Trading Economics. Looking back, in the year of 1945, Slovakia had a population of 3.4 million people. source: Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic

personal-income-tax-rate.description: The Personal Income Tax Rate in Slovakia stands at 35 percent.
